name: clang
title: clang
summary: C, C++ and Objective-C compiler (LLVM based), clang binary
description:
C, C++ and Objective-C compiler (LLVM based), clang binary
Clang project is a C, C++, Objective C and Objective C++ front-end
for the LLVM compiler. Its goal is to offer a replacement to the GNU Compiler
Collection (GCC).
.
Clang implements all of the ISO C++ 1998, 11, 14 and 17 standards and also
provides most of the support of C++20.
.
This is a dependency package providing the default clang compiler.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use clang because C, C++ and Objective-C compiler (LLVM based), clang binary.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use clang as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `clang [options] filename ...` as documented upstream for clang.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair clang with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `clang --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: clang - the Clang C, C++, and Objective-C compiler
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install llvm-defaults (0.71). Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `clang [options] filename ...`. C, C++ and Objective-C compiler (LLVM based), clang binary.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: C, C++ and Objective-C compiler (LLVM based), clang binary.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: clang - the Clang C, C++, and Objective-C compiler.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install llvm-defaults (0.71)
Install with: sudo apt install llvm-defaults (0.71)
